suggestions.name: Brooks Ghost
suggestions.note: Reliable daily trainer — soft, forgiving, great for beginners.

suggestions.name: ASICS Gel-Nimbus
suggestions.note: Extra cushioning if you want maximum impact protection.

suggestions.name: Saucony Ride
suggestions.note: Balanced feel — not too soft, not too firm. Good all-rounder.

suggestions.name: New Balance Fresh Foam 880
suggestions.note: Comfortable neutral shoe; wide widths available.

womenSuggestions.name: Brooks Ghost (women's)
womenSuggestions.note: Same forgiving ride with a women's-specific last — try both standard and wide.

womenSuggestions.name: ASICS Gel-Cumulus (women's)
womenSuggestions.note: Soft daily trainer; popular for higher arches and narrower heels.

womenSuggestions.name: New Balance Fresh Foam 880 (wide)
womenSuggestions.note: Extra forefoot room if standard women's widths feel tight.

womenSuggestions.name: Hoka Clifton (women's)
womenSuggestions.note: Plush cushioning with a roomy toe box — good for longer easy miles.

suggestions.name: Nike Dri-FIT Miler
suggestions.note: Classic beginner top — light, breathable, affordable.

suggestions.name: Under Armour Tech 2.0
suggestions.note: Soft feel, good value, widely available.

suggestions.name: Patagonia Capilene Cool
suggestions.note: Great for hot weather and sustainability-minded runners.

suggestions.name: Merino base layer (Icebreaker / Smartwool)
suggestions.note: Odour-resistant and comfortable for cool-weather runs.

womenSuggestions.name: Brooks Drive 3 Pocket Run Bra
womenSuggestions.note: High-support sports bra with a phone pocket — reduces bounce on easy and long runs.

womenSuggestions.name: Lululemon Energy Longline Bra
womenSuggestions.note: Medium support for smaller frames; comfortable under tanks.

womenSuggestions.name: Nike Dri-FIT Swoosh Women's Tank
womenSuggestions.note: Light, breezy top with a flattering cut — easy first technical shirt.

womenSuggestions.name: Oiselle Flyout Tank
womenSuggestions.note: Women's running brand — soft fabric and a relaxed race-day feel.

suggestions.name: Nike Challenger Shorts
suggestions.note: Simple 5" inseam with liner — a safe first pick.

suggestions.name: Tracksmith Session Shorts
suggestions.note: Premium feel with a classic look if you want to invest.

suggestions.name: Lululemon Pace Breaker
suggestions.note: Comfortable liner and phone pocket; popular for everyday runs.

suggestions.name: SOAR Running Split Shorts
suggestions.note: Light and fast — better once you're running regularly.

womenSuggestions.name: Lululemon Hotty Hot High-Rise
womenSuggestions.note: Light 4" short with liner and pockets — a common women's starter pick.

womenSuggestions.name: Oiselle Roga Short
womenSuggestions.note: Women's-specific cut with a comfortable brief liner.

womenSuggestions.name: Athleta Ultimate Lined Short
womenSuggestions.note: Mid-rise option with a secure waistband for longer runs.

womenSuggestions.name: Nike Tempo Women's Short
womenSuggestions.note: Classic curved-hem running short — widely available and affordable.

suggestions.name: Nike Pro Warm Tights
suggestions.note: Solid cold-weather staple with good warmth-to-weight ratio.

suggestions.name: Lululemon Fast and Free Tight
suggestions.note: Multiple pockets and a comfortable high waist.

suggestions.name: Under Armour ColdGear Leggings
suggestions.note: Brushed interior for winter mornings.

suggestions.name: 2XU Compression Tights
suggestions.note: Snug fit — some runners like the supported feel on long runs.

womenSuggestions.name: Lululemon Fast and Free High-Rise Tight
womenSuggestions.note: High waist stays put; multiple pockets for phone and gels.

womenSuggestions.name: Athleta Ultimate Stash Tight
womenSuggestions.note: Soft, squat-proof fabric with side pockets — great for cold commutes.

womenSuggestions.name: Sweaty Betty Power Leggings
womenSuggestions.note: Comfortable compression feel without feeling restrictive.

womenSuggestions.name: Brooks Method 7/8 Tight
womenSuggestions.note: Running-specific fit with reflective details for dawn and dusk.

suggestions.name: Ciele GOCap
suggestions.note: The cult favourite — light, stylish, great sweat management.

suggestions.name: Nike Featherlight Cap
suggestions.note: Budget-friendly and does the job well.

suggestions.name: Buff UV Headband (winter alternative)
suggestions.note: Covers ears in cold weather without overheating your head.

suggestions.name: Smartwool Merino Beanie
suggestions.note: For freezing temps when a cap isn't enough.

womenSuggestions.name: Ciele TRKCap
womenSuggestions.note: Ponytail-friendly opening in back — no awkward bun stuffing.

womenSuggestions.name: Nike AeroBill Featherlight (women's)
womenSuggestions.note: Light cap with a lower profile that fits smaller head sizes.

womenSuggestions.name: Buff Coolnet UV+ Headband
womenSuggestions.note: Keeps hair off your face on hot runs without full hat hair.

womenSuggestions.name: Smartwool Merino Headband
womenSuggestions.note: Covers ears in winter while leaving room for a ponytail or braid.

suggestions.name: Goodr
suggestions.note: Affordable, fun colours, no-slip fit — perfect starter shades.

suggestions.name: Tifosi Swank
suggestions.note: Light, budget-friendly, solid UV protection.

suggestions.name: Oakley Sutro Lite
suggestions.note: Premium option with excellent clarity and stability.

suggestions.name: Julbo Fury
suggestions.note: Great for trail runners who want wraparound coverage.

womenSuggestions.name: Goodr Circle G
womenSuggestions.note: Smaller frame and nose bridge — fits narrower faces without sliding.

womenSuggestions.name: Tifosi Swank (XS)
womenSuggestions.note: Extra-small size option for petite faces.

womenSuggestions.name: Oakley Revolver
womenSuggestions.note: Women's-specific fit with secure temples for sweaty miles.

womenSuggestions.name: 100% Speedcraft SL (narrow)
womenSuggestions.note: Lightweight with a slimmer temple if standard sport frames feel wide.

suggestions.name: Neutrogena Sport Face SPF 70
suggestions.note: Sweat-resistant and less likely to sting eyes than body formulas.

suggestions.name: Banana Boat Sport Ultra SPF 50
suggestions.note: Budget-friendly, widely available, solid for arms and legs.

suggestions.name: Supergoop! Play Everyday Lotion SPF 50
suggestions.note: Light feel, no white cast — good for daily training.

suggestions.name: Sun Bum Original SPF 50
suggestions.note: Popular sport formula with a pleasant scent; reapply on long runs.

womenSuggestions.name: EltaMD UV Clear SPF 46
womenSuggestions.note: Lightweight face formula — popular under ponytails and visors.

womenSuggestions.name: Supergoop! Unseen Sunscreen SPF 40
womenSuggestions.note: Invisible finish that won't clash with sports bra straps or tank lines.

womenSuggestions.name: Neutrogena Sport Face Oil-Free SPF 70
womenSuggestions.note: Sweat-resistant without a greasy feel on forehead and shoulders.

womenSuggestions.name: Banana Boat Light as Air SPF 50
womenSuggestions.note: Thin body lotion for arms and legs — absorbs quickly before you lace up.

suggestions.name: Body Glide
suggestions.note: The classic stick — apply to thighs, feet, and anywhere that rubs.

suggestions.name: Squirrel's Nut Butter
suggestions.note: Natural anti-chafe balm; popular with trail runners.

suggestions.name: Bandelettes or Nip Guards
suggestions.note: Physical barriers for nipple chafing on long runs.

suggestions.name: Vaseline
suggestions.note: Cheap and works in a pinch — not ideal for fabric stains.

womenSuggestions.name: Body Glide (sports bra band & underarms)
womenSuggestions.note: Apply along bra band, straps, and underarm seams before long runs.

womenSuggestions.name: Shefit Ultimate Sports Bra
womenSuggestions.note: Adjustable zip-front bra — dial in support to cut bounce-related rubbing.

womenSuggestions.name: Bandelettes thigh bands
womenSuggestions.note: Lace-free barrier when shorts liners aren't enough for thigh rub.

womenSuggestions.name: Monistat Chafing Relief Powder Gel
womenSuggestions.note: A silicone anti-chafe gel (not an antifungal medication) many runners use along bra lines and inner thighs. Patch-test first if you have sensitive skin.

suggestions.name: Nuun Sport
suggestions.note: Light, fizzy, easy on the stomach — great starter electrolyte.

suggestions.name: LMNT
suggestions.note: Higher sodium — popular for heavy sweaters and hot climates.

suggestions.name: Precision Fuel & Hydration
suggestions.note: Customizable sodium levels to match your sweat rate.

suggestions.name: SaltStick FastChews
suggestions.note: Chewable electrolytes if you prefer not to mix drinks.

womenSuggestions.name: Nuun Sport (lower-sugar flavours)
womenSuggestions.note: Gentle on the stomach — useful if gels and sweet drinks feel heavy.

womenSuggestions.name: Precision Fuel PF 30 Electrolyte
womenSuggestions.note: Sweat-test kits help match sodium needs across cycle phases.

womenSuggestions.name: SaltStick FastChews
womenSuggestions.note: Easy to dose on long runs without carrying a full bottle mix.

womenSuggestions.name: LMNT (half packet in 16 oz)
womenSuggestions.note: High sodium for heavy sweaters — start with a lighter mix if new to electrolytes.

suggestions.name: Maurten Gel 100
suggestions.note: Gentle on the stomach — popular with marathoners.

suggestions.name: GU Energy Gel
suggestions.note: Wide flavour range; easy to find at races and shops.

suggestions.name: Huma Gel
suggestions.note: Made with chia and fruit — good if standard gels upset your gut.

suggestions.name: Spring Energy
suggestions.note: Whole-food style fuel for runners who want real-ingredient options.

womenSuggestions.name: Huma Gel
womenSuggestions.note: Real-food ingredients — often easier on sensitive stomachs.

womenSuggestions.name: Maurten Gel 100
womenSuggestions.note: Lower osmolality can sit better during hard efforts.

womenSuggestions.name: GU Roctane (caffeine-free flavours)
womenSuggestions.note: Test caffeine-free first if you're sensitive during your cycle.

womenSuggestions.name: Spring Energy Canaberry
womenSuggestions.note: Mild, fruit-forward taste — good starter gel for long runs.

suggestions.name: Salomon Active Skin 8
suggestions.note: Light race vest — popular for half and full marathon training.

suggestions.name: Nathan VaporHowe 2.0
suggestions.note: Comfortable women's-specific fit with good storage.

suggestions.name: Osprey Dyna LT
suggestions.note: Hybrid pack/vest — great for trail beginners.

suggestions.name: FlipBelt or handheld bottle
suggestions.note: Simpler option for shorter long runs — no vest needed yet.

womenSuggestions.name: Nathan VaporHowe 2.0
womenSuggestions.note: Women's-specific cut — shorter torso and shaped sternum straps.

womenSuggestions.name: Osprey Dyna 15 (women's)
womenSuggestions.note: Hydration pack sized for narrower shoulders and shorter backs.

womenSuggestions.name: Salomon Active Skin 8 (S/M women's)
womenSuggestions.note: Race vest with front flasks — check size chart for bust fit.

womenSuggestions.name: FlipBelt
womenSuggestions.note: Waist belt option if vests chafe under sports bra bands.

suggestions.name: Apple Watch SE (2nd gen)
suggestions.note: Best value — full GPS running features without the premium price.

suggestions.name: Apple Watch Series 9 / 10
suggestions.note: Brighter always-on display; easier to read mid-run.

suggestions.name: Apple Watch Ultra
suggestions.note: Overkill for beginners — consider only for trail ultras and multi-day battery needs.

womenSuggestions.name: Apple Watch SE (40mm)
womenSuggestions.note: Smaller case fits narrower wrists without overpowering your arm.

womenSuggestions.name: Apple Watch Series 9 / 10 (41mm)
womenSuggestions.note: Compact size with a brighter screen for glancing at pace mid-run.

womenSuggestions.name: Sport Loop or Solo Loop (small)
womenSuggestions.note: Flexible bands that fit smaller wrists without extra tail flapping.

suggestions.name: Garmin Forerunner 165
suggestions.note: Colour AMOLED screen, beginner-friendly — great first Garmin.

suggestions.name: Garmin Forerunner 255 / 265
suggestions.note: The sweet spot for half and full marathon training.

suggestions.name: Garmin Forerunner 55
suggestions.note: Cheapest entry point — basic but reliable GPS tracking.

womenSuggestions.name: Garmin Forerunner 165
womenSuggestions.note: 43mm case suits smaller wrists; quick-release bands for a secure fit.

womenSuggestions.name: Garmin Forerunner 265S
womenSuggestions.note: Smaller "S" size — same features with a narrower case and band.

womenSuggestions.name: Garmin silicone band (small)
womenSuggestions.note: Stock bands run long — size down so the watch doesn't slide on sweaty runs.

suggestions.name: Coros Pace 3
suggestions.note: Best beginner pick — light, accurate GPS, weeks of battery.

suggestions.name: Coros Pace 2
suggestions.note: Still solid if you find it discounted — slightly older but capable.

suggestions.name: Coros Apex 2
suggestions.note: Step up for trail runners who want maps and sapphire glass.

womenSuggestions.name: Coros Pace 3
womenSuggestions.note: Light 30g case works well on smaller wrists — silicone band in S/M.

womenSuggestions.name: Coros Pace 3 nylon band
womenSuggestions.note: Softer, lower-profile strap if the stock band feels stiff or long.

womenSuggestions.name: Coros Apex 2 (42mm)
womenSuggestions.note: Smaller case option if you want maps without a bulky watch face.

suggestions.name: Phone-only tracking
suggestions.note: Start here — just open Strava and hit Record before your run.

suggestions.name: Strava + Apple Watch / Garmin / Coros
suggestions.note: Record on your watch; sync automatically when done.

suggestions.name: LetsRunNow dashboard
suggestions.note: Connect Strava in your account for streaks, route comparisons, and HR tips.

womenSuggestions.name: FlipBelt or armband (phone carry)
womenSuggestions.note: If you run phone-only, waist belts often sit better over high-rise shorts than pockets.

womenSuggestions.name: Strava privacy zones
womenSuggestions.note: Hide start/end near home — useful if you often run solo from the same address.

womenSuggestions.name: Strava + sports watch (40–43mm)
womenSuggestions.note: Smaller watches pair the same way — record on wrist, review on phone after.
